heaviest_rainfall.status: The heaviest total rainfall occurred on 09 November 2025, dropping 47.89mm of rain over the 144.8 hour storm duration with a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: The longest rain storm on record lasted 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm of rain fell and at one point was falling at 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since 11 April 2025, the day with the most lightning strikes is 07 September 2025 when 23 were seen.

storm.status: An active storm has been detected in the vicinity.  The last storm experienced was on 07 January 2026.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not a cold snap right now.  There have been 1 this year, compared to a usual average of 3.6.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C) on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: At the moment, it is not a heatwaves.  This year has seen 0 heatwavess; typically, 1.9 heatwavess happen every year.

tropical.status: The last observed tropical night was 18 July 2025, making a total of only 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (227°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The top recorded wind gust is 120.4 mph on 08 January 2026.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: To date, the maximum wind speed recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the outside temperature (down 2.8°C), feels like temperature (down 2.3°C), and inside temperature (down 2.1°C) and drier conditions in rainfall (down 0.2 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in pressure (up 8 mbar) and rainfall (up 0.2 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in dew point temperature (down 1.9°C), rainfall (down 0.8 mm), and cloud levels (up 382 m) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 20.3m higher than yesterday, 382.0m lower than the past week, 359.1m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 492m to a maximum of 831m, averaging 713.2m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 1095.2m and 1072.2m respectively.

summary.summary: Inside; comfortable and pleasant temperatures. Outside; freezing temperatures are anticipated. Also, dry air conditions likely prevail and little or no wind. Possible light showers, cloud cover will be low, possibly causing overcast or foggy conditions, and it’s dim outside. Low pressure may lead to stormy or rainy weather.
